message:
fix(core): correctness and robustness improvements across layout, render, runtime, PTY, and TTY

A broad sweep of correctness fixes, numerical stability improvements,
and defense-in-depth hardening across the core FrankenTUI crates. Each
change addresses a specific failure mode discovered through testing,
fuzzing, or code review.

ftui-core/input_parser.rs — Complete Ctrl+punctuation coverage:

The input parser handled Ctrl+A through Ctrl+Z (0x01-0x1A) but silently
dropped Ctrl+\, Ctrl+], Ctrl+^, and Ctrl+_ (0x1C-0x1F). These are
valid terminal control sequences used by tools like Vim (Ctrl+]) and
signal handlers (Ctrl+\). Added explicit match arms for all four.

ftui-extras/diagram_layout.rs — Eliminate per-call allocation in barycenter sort:

barycenter_sort() allocated a fresh Vec<(usize, f64)> on every
invocation. Since minimize_crossings() calls it hundreds of times per
layout pass, this created significant allocation pressure. The fix
promotes the scratch buffer to the caller scope and passes it as a
&mut parameter, reusing the allocation across all calls. Also fixed
the cycle_removal_multi_cycle test which had incorrect edge definitions
and corrected the reverse-adjacency list in the crossing minimization
test to match the forward adjacency list.

ftui-extras/visual_fx.rs — Fix vignette aspect ratio distortion:

The Scrim vignette effect divided by cx/cy (half-width/height) to
normalize coordinates, but when cx or cy was 0 (single-row or
single-column region), this produced NaN. The comment said
"normalized distance in [0,1]" but the actual range was [-1,1]. The
fix uses .max(1.0) to avoid division by zero and adds a clarifying
comment about aspect-ratio normalization.

ftui-layout/lib.rs — Fix layout rounding edge cases:

round_layout_stable() used a while loop distributing deficit one round
at a time, but when deficit >= n (number of items), the loop ran
ceil(deficit/n) iterations doing essentially the same work. The fix
handles the large-deficit case up front with integer division, then
distributes the remainder in a single pass via Hamilton's method.

redistribute_overflow() could enter an infinite loop when the maximum
value in the result was 0 but overflow was still positive (impossible
for valid inputs, but reachable via fuzz testing). Added a hard
fallback that zeros all values and breaks. Also simplified the
arithmetic by replacing manual ceiling division with .div_ceil() and
eliminated unnecessary dereferencing patterns.

ftui-layout/pane.rs — Fix overflow in coordinate scaling:

scale_div_round() multiplied two i64 values together, which could
overflow on large coordinates (e.g., pane normalization with 4K+
terminal sizes and high DPI scaling factors). The fix widens to i128
for the intermediate multiplication, then narrows back to i64 via
try_into() with an explicit overflow error. The separate div_round()
function now delegates to scale_div_round() with numerator=1 to
eliminate code duplication.

ftui-pty/virtual_terminal.rs — Tab handling and test accuracy:

Tab processing crashed with an out-of-bounds access when cursor_x
was already at or past the terminal width. Added a bounds check that
clamps cursor_x before scanning for the next tab stop. Updated
several test cases to use more precise assertions and added
assert_invariants() calls to ICH, DCH, ECH, IL, DL, REP, and
scrollback tests to catch wide-character consistency violations.

ftui-render/presenter.rs — Bounded wide-char tail repair:

The presenter's wide-character tail repair logic only handled direct
chars (not grapheme-pool references) to avoid accidentally erasing
content after wide grapheme payloads. However, this left width-2
grapheme-pool entries (the most common case: CJK characters interned
in the pool) without repair, causing orphaned continuation cells to
appear as garbage. The fix extends repair to width-2 grapheme refs
(where the repair scope is bounded to a single cell) while still
excluding wider payloads. Also added a width-consistency check when
emitting grapheme content: if sanitization changes the display width,
the presenter falls back to placeholder characters to maintain cursor
synchronization. Fixed cursor sync logic to handle the case where
cursor_x is None (no known position) by forcing a position reset.

ftui-render/spatial_hit_index.rs — Eliminate intermediate allocation:

The bucket rebuild in rebuild_from_entries() collected all (index, rect)
pairs into a Vec before iterating, solely to avoid a borrow conflict.
The fix iterates by index instead, reading each rect individually,
which avoids the temporary allocation entirely.

ftui-runtime/allocation_budget.rs — Log-space E-process for numerical stability:

The E-process wealth tracker multiplied e_value by exp(log_increment)
each frame, which accumulated floating-point error over thousands of
frames and could overflow to infinity during sustained allocation
spikes. The fix stores the E-process value in log-space
(log_e_value += log_increment instead of e_value *= exp(increment)),
converting back via .exp() only when needed for threshold comparison
or external reporting. Also added input validation for sigma_sq,
lambda, and alpha (must be finite, alpha must be in [0,1)).

ftui-runtime/program.rs — Wire RuntimeLane into task executor backend:

ProgramConfig.runtime_lane was previously informational — it logged
which lane was active but didn't influence the task executor backend
selection. Callers had to manually set EffectQueueConfig.with_backend()
to match their lane choice, which was error-prone and led to the
default ProgramConfig using the legacy spawned backend even when the
default lane was Structured.

The fix adds resolved_effect_queue_config() which checks whether the
caller explicitly set a backend (tracked via explicit_backend flag).
If not, it resolves the backend from the lane: Legacy -> Spawned,
Structured -> EffectQueue, Asupersync -> Asupersync (with feature
gate fallback to EffectQueue). This means
ProgramConfig::default().run() now uses the effect queue instead of
spawned threads, matching the Structured lane default.

ftui-runtime/resize_coalescer.rs — Simplify duration arithmetic:

Replaced checked_duration_since().unwrap_or(Duration::ZERO) with
saturating_duration_since(), which does the same thing in one call.
Replaced manual if elapsed >= target { ZERO } else { target - elapsed }
patterns with saturating_sub(). Fixed the time_until_next_apply()
method to return Duration::ZERO instead of the hard deadline when no
events have been received yet (previously fell through to the hard
deadline, which was incorrect because the coalescer should apply
immediately when an event arrives with no prior event). Several tests
were updated to use handle_resize_at + tick_at with explicit
timestamps instead of handle_resize + implicit Instant::now(), because
the latter was racy under CI load. Also fixed property tests where
resize events used the same height value, causing the coalescer to
treat them as no-ops (duplicate size) and skip coalescing.

ftui-runtime/terminal_writer.rs — Robust cleanup and fault tolerance:

clear_screen() previously just sent ESC[2J ESC[1;1H and flushed,
ignoring any active scroll region, saved cursor state, or open sync
block. If any of these were active, the clear would operate within
the scroll region (not the full screen), the saved cursor would become
stale, and the sync block would remain open. The fix resets all three
before clearing, preserving the first error encountered and continuing
best-effort through the remaining cleanup steps.

The inline cursor positioning now bounds-checks cx/cy against the
buffer dimensions before emitting a cursor-move sequence, preventing
the cursor from being positioned outside the visible region.

The 3-buffer rotation (prev_buffer advancement) now only occurs after
a successful present. Previously, a partial write failure would advance
the baseline, causing the next frame's diff to miss the cells that
were never actually written to the terminal.

The inline-active widget gauge (INLINE_ACTIVE_WIDGETS) was moved from
Ordering::Relaxed to Ordering::SeqCst and relocated to execute before
any fallible operations, ensuring the increment always has a matching
decrement in Drop even if construction fails partway through.

Alt-screen and inline cleanup paths now emit SGR_BG_DEFAULT before
erase operations to prevent background color leakage during teardown.

Added SingleWriteFaultWriter test helper that injects a write failure
on a specific call number, enabling tests that verify the writer
recovers correctly from partial-write faults.

ftui-tty/lib.rs — RAII signal intercept guard and EOF spin prevention:

Signal interception registration used a manual boolean flag that was
decremented in a separate error-handling branch, creating a risk of
counter leak if a new error path was added. The fix introduces
SignalInterceptGuard with Drop-based cleanup, ensuring the session
counter is always decremented. The guard has a disarm() method for
the success path where ownership transfers to TtyBackend.

TtyEventSource::pump_ready_bytes() now retires the reader on EOF by
setting tty_reader to None. Previously, EOF left the fd alive, causing
poll() to wake immediately on POLLHUP in a tight spin loop until the
outer deadline expired. The fix also adds a short-circuit in
poll_event(): if the reader is retired and no parser timeout state is
pending, return false immediately instead of entering the poll loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -305,6 +305,19 @@ impl InputParser {
                     KeyEvent::new(KeyCode::Char(c)).with_modifiers(Modifiers::CTRL),
                 ))
             }
+            // Ctrl+\, Ctrl+], Ctrl+^, Ctrl+_ (0x1C-0x1F)
+            0x1C => Some(Event::Key(
+                KeyEvent::new(KeyCode::Char('\\')).with_modifiers(Modifiers::CTRL),
+            )),
+            0x1D => Some(Event::Key(
+                KeyEvent::new(KeyCode::Char(']')).with_modifiers(Modifiers::CTRL),
+            )),
+            0x1E => Some(Event::Key(
+                KeyEvent::new(KeyCode::Char('^')).with_modifiers(Modifiers::CTRL),
+            )),
+            0x1F => Some(Event::Key(
+                KeyEvent::new(KeyCode::Char('_')).with_modifiers(Modifiers::CTRL),
+            )),
             // Backspace (DEL)
             0x7F => Some(Event::Key(KeyEvent::new(KeyCode::Backspace))),
             // Printable ASCII
